title: Iron Snake Mode: Random Board Shapes & Level Goals
content:
## Overview

Iron Snake Mode is an optional game mode (default off) where the board is carved into a random, irregular shape instead of the usual square grid. The grid-doubling mechanic (`expandGrid`) is replaced by a level/goal system: each level has a cumulative score threshold; when reached, a fresh random shape is generated, snakes are repositioned, and a higher goal is set. The mode composes with all three existing game modes (single-player, two-player, bot-vs-bot).

## Activation & Naming

A toggle in the menu button group (below "Bot vs Bot", above the footer) enables the mode. The label reads **Iron Snake Mode**. The name went through two earlier iterations ("Random Arena" → "Iron Man Mode" → "Iron Snake Mode"); all internal identifiers were renamed in one pass from `arena`/`randomArena` to `ironSnake`/`ironSnakeMode` to match, confirmed clean with `grep -i arena`.

## Files

| File | Role |
|---|---|
| `src/iron-snake.ts` | Board mask generator (pure, no game state) |
| `src/main.ts` | State fields, `onBoard`, `inBounds`, `findStartCell`, level/goal logic, food/HUD/render guards |
| `index.html` | Menu toggle, HUD countdown element |
| `src/style.css` | Toggle styling |

## Board Shape Generation (`src/iron-snake.ts`)

**Algorithm: overlapping rooms.** Several rectangles (minimum side 3) are placed so each new room overlaps an existing on-board cell, guaranteeing connectivity by construction. After placement:

1. **Flood fill** — keep only the largest 4-connected component.
2. **Area check** — reject if fill ratio is outside `[targetLo, targetHi]`.
3. **Min-width check** — morphological open with a 3×3 element (erode then dilate). If the open removes any cell, that cell sat in a corridor narrower than 3 cells → reject.
4. **Retry** — up to 30 attempts; returns `null` on exhaustion so callers always fall back to a full rectangle.

Exported as `generateIronSnakeMask(gridSize, rng?) → { mask: Uint8Array; area: number } | null`. Verified across 8,000 trials with 100% connectivity and zero corridor failures.

The mask is a flat `Uint8Array` of length `gridSize²` (`1` = playable, `0` = off-board). Index lookup `mask[y * gridSize + x]` is used rather than a `Set<string>` because `inBounds` runs in tight BFS loops.

## Collision Architecture (`src/main.ts`)

`inBounds()` is the single collision chokepoint — player collision, multiplayer wall checks, `wouldCollide`, `checkCollision`, and bot BFS (`analyzePosition`) all funnel through it.

A new helper `onBoard(x, y)` is the single source of truth for playable cells:

```
onBoard(x, y): rectangle bounds AND (boardMask === null OR boardMask[y*gridSize + x] === 1)
```

`inBounds()` is rewritten to delegate entirely to `onBoard()`. This means collision detection, food spawning, and bot pathfinding automatically respect the irregular shape with **no changes to bot logic or movement loops**. Classic mode keeps `boardMask = null` and `inBounds` short-circuits to the original rectangle check.

## State Fields

- `boardMask: Uint8Array | null` — `null` in classic mode.
- `ironSnakeMode: boolean` — the menu toggle; persists across replays.
- `boardArea: number` — count of playable cells (used by food spawning instead of `gridSize²`).
- `levelGoal: number` — current cumulative score threshold.

## Level & Goal System

Replaces `checkGridExpansion()` when Iron Snake Mode is active. The dispatcher at the two `checkGridExpansion()` call sites routes to `checkLevelAdvance()` instead.

**Goal formula:** `5·L(L+1)/2`, producing cumulative thresholds of **5, 15, 30, 50, 75…** with per-level deltas of 5, 10, 15, 20, 25… — each level strictly harder than the previous.

Goals are thresholds on the **running total score** (not per-level resets), so the leaderboard score is always the honest final score and each threshold is crossed exactly once.

**Level advance trigger:**
- Single-player: `score >= levelGoal`
- Multiplayer: `score + score2 >= levelGoal`

**`advanceLevel()` sequence:**
1. `level++`
2. Regenerate mask (fall back to `null` on failure)
3. Reposition snake(s) to length 1 via `findStartCell`
4. `spawnFood()`
5. `levelGoal = goalForLevel(level)`
6. Ramp speed gently: `baseSpeed * 0.9^(level-1)`, floored (10% per level, not halving)
7. Restart the game interval
8. `updateUI()`

`gridSize` does **not** change in Iron Snake Mode, so the canvas never resizes and `isoCache` stays valid.

## Snake Placement (`findStartCell`)

`findStartCell(preferredRegion, occupied) → { pos, dir }` picks an on-board unoccupied cell nearest the preferred region with a direction giving ≥ `FORWARD_CLEARANCE` (~3) clear cells ahead, preventing immediate wall collisions.

- Single-player / level advance: `findStartCell(centroid, ∅)`
- Multiplayer P1: left-third centroid; P2: right-third centroid with P1 cells marked occupied
- Mutual reachability is guaranteed — the mask is always a single connected component

Falls back to classic rectangle placement if `findStartCell` returns null.

## HUD

When Iron Snake Mode is active, the grid-size display is replaced by a live countdown: **"To goal: N pts / 1 pt"** that decrements as the player eats and resets on level advance. This works for both single-player and the multiplayer combined-score case with no extra UI.

## Food Spawning

`spawnFood` guards both the empty-cell filter and the rejection-sample `while` condition with `onBoard`, and uses `boardArea` (not `gridSize²`) as the branch selector for the high-fill path. The empty-list case is guarded.

## Rendering

- `drawGroundPlane`: skips cells where `!onBoard(gx, gy)` — void cells show the dark background.
- `drawGridLines`: when mask is active, strokes per-cell edges (deduplicating shared edges) instead of full spans.
- `drawGroundBorder`: when mask is active, strokes every edge where an on-board cell meets an off-board cell or the rectangle boundary, tracing the true irregular outline.
- `drawBlock` / `getBlockHeight` and `isoCache` are unchanged — the cache covers the full `(gridSize+1)²` lattice and is valid for any subset.

## Bots

Bots are **shape-unaware** — `BotState` (`bot-types.ts`) is unchanged. Bot moves are validated through the mask-aware `inBounds`, so a stale heuristic kills the bot rather than corrupting state. Edge-hugging bots (explorer, edge-runner, spiral, sweeper, ambusher, zigzag) degrade gracefully on irregular shapes. This is accepted behavior.

## Leaderboard

Iron Snake Mode runs record into the same `single|pvp|bvb` leaderboard boards (`leaderboard.ts` is untouched). Tagging entries `ironSnake: true` is a possible follow-up.
